word: G4382 - προσωποληψία - prosōpolēpsía - pros-o-pol-ape-see'-ah - Respect of persons

sections.paragraph:
- Original: προσωποληψία

- Transliteration: Prosopolepsia

- Phonetic: pros-o-pol-ape-see'-ah

- Definition:

1. respect of persons

2. partiality

a. the fault of one who when called on to give judgment has respect of the outward circumstances of man and not to their intrinsic merits, and so prefers, as the more worthy, one who is rich, high born, or powerful, to another who does not have these qualities

- Origin: from G4381

- TDNT entry: 19:00,0

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Feminine

- Strong's: From G4381; partiality that is favoritism: - respect of persons.
